source: Physics-derived oa-TOF project baseline; solver and CAD implementations must conform to this contract
naming_convention: Code variables use <component>_<feature>_<quantity>_<unit>; global positions include the axis (for example accelerator_grid2_z_mm), local coordinates include local, counts end in _count, voltages end in _v. Keys inside geometry_mm inherit millimetres from the section name.

coordinate_convention.origin: detector active-plane center and exact first-order accelerator time-focus plane
coordinate_convention.software_contract: COMSOL and CAD use this global frame directly; SIMION PA files may use local coordinates only when their IOB instance transforms map exactly into this frame

accelerator.rule: Keep d1=3.0 mm; set d2=16.8 mm so all six grid1-to-grid2 pitches are 2.8 mm; retain voltages; translate the accelerator and source rigidly so the derived first-order global focus exactly matches the former 16.83 mm engineering baseline

reflectron.rule: U1=2*U0*(L+2*d1)/(3*L); E2=12*U0*(sqrt(3)*sqrt(L)+sqrt(L-4*d1))/(sqrt(3)*L^1.5+8*sqrt(3)*sqrt(L)*d1+3*L*sqrt(L-4*d1)); d2min=(U0-U1)/E2; d2_raw=d2min*(1+margin); V_mirror=U1+E2*d2_raw; L_reflectron_raw=d1+d2_raw; only after all physics values are derived, round engineering lengths and voltages to their declared precisions

grid_policy.baseline: one-grid-unit-thick full-aperture transparent ideal electrode sheet
grid_policy.nonideal_grid: separate high-resolution local wire-cell model plus transmission/kick map
grid_policy.forbidden_baseline_approximation: solid plate with a large aperture
